sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.promptHint: 3–5 sentences: the decision needed, the recommended action, and the financial case in one or two key numbers (e.g. 'projected 3-year ROI of 240%, payback in 14 months'). Executives read this first — make the recommendation clear.
sections.ragQuery: initiative decision recommendation investment return outcome

sections.title: Problem Statement
sections.promptHint: Describe the business problem or opportunity in concrete terms. Include: the current state, the cost of inaction (quantified where possible), and why this needs to be addressed now.
sections.ragQuery: problem challenge current state pain cost of inaction

sections.title: Proposed Solution
sections.promptHint: Describe what is being invested in and how it addresses the problem. Be specific about scope, timeline, and key implementation steps. Do not over-engineer the description — focus on what, not how.
sections.ragQuery: solution approach implementation scope timeline deliverables

sections.title: Financial Analysis
sections.promptHint: Include in table format: Total Cost (one-time + ongoing by category), Expected Return (revenue gained, cost avoided, or time saved in $ value), ROI ((Return - Cost) / Cost), Payback Period, and 3-year NPV if multi-year. State all assumptions explicitly.
sections.ragQuery: cost budget investment return ROI savings financial projections

sections.title: Risk Assessment
sections.promptHint: List the top 3–5 risks. For each: description, probability (High/Med/Low), impact (High/Med/Low), and mitigation plan. Use a table. Include the risk of doing nothing as the first row.
sections.ragQuery: risks challenges concerns failure modes mitigation

sections.title: Alternatives Considered
sections.promptHint: List 2–3 alternatives that were evaluated. For each: brief description, key advantages, and why it was not recommended. This demonstrates the recommendation was reached through analysis, not assumption.

sections.title: Recommendation
sections.promptHint: State the clear decision recommendation. If conditional (e.g. proceed only if X), state the conditions. Include: next steps, who needs to approve, and the cost of delay if the decision is deferred.
